- Why this change:

## UX Consistency Checklist

- [ ] I used existing shared primitives/components (`src/index.css`, `src/components/ui`) instead of introducing one-off styles where equivalents exist.
- [ ] Any new visual pattern was added as a reusable primitive first, then adopted by the feature/page.
- [ ] Responsive behavior was verified for desktop and mobile breakpoints.
- [ ] Keyboard accessibility and relevant ARIA semantics were validated for interactive controls.
- [ ] RTL/LTR behavior was verified for user-facing changes.
- [ ] I checked for overflow/layout regressions in changed views.
- [ ] I confirmed reduced-motion/device-constrained experiences remain acceptable.

## Validation

- [ ] `npm run build`
- [ ] Targeted tests executed (list below)

## Release Impact

- [ ] If this change is part of a release candidate, I updated the `Release Checklist` issue and attached current release-control evidence.
